content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 5, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Today saw significant discussion around agent identity and capability enforcement, with a new issue proposing an economy for AI agents. A key issue regarding a non-custodial wallet plugin was closed.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No new features, bug fixes, code refactoring, documentation enhancements, or tests were added today.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
No new pull requests were opened today.

## CLOSED ISSUES

### Agent Financial Capabilities
The issue concerning a non-custodial wallet plugin, `elizaos-plugin-agentwallet`, was closed today [#6552](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6552). This plugin provides ElizaOS agents with financial capabilities including checking balances, transferring tokens, swapping, and bridging.

### Agent-to-Agent Messaging Strategy
The feature request for Cross-Agent Messaging was closed as "not planned" [#6418](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6418). The rationale is that wallet-native identity, particularly through XMTP protocol integration, is seen as the foundational solution for agent-to-agent coordination and cryptographic identity, rather than a separate messaging system.

## NEW ISSUES

### AI Agent Economy Proposal
A new issue, "AIGEN Protocol — Earn $AIGEN tokens by contributing AI agent tools," was opened [#6708](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6708). This proposes an economy where ElizaOS agents can earn $AIGEN tokens for contributing value, such as using SafeAgent Shield for safe crypto trading.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Agent Identity and Trust Layer
Discussion continued on the "Plugin Proposal: AgentID — Cryptographic Identity & Trust Layer for ElizaOS Agents" issue [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6688). @haroldmalikfrimpong-ops provided corrections regarding the dynamic nature of AgentID's trust levels (L1-L4 capability-based, not static L0-L4) and the server-side auto-detection of behavioral changes. They also highlighted their anomaly detection mechanisms and offered to compare evidence schemas, noting their receipts are JCS RFC 8785 canonicalized and Ed25519 dual-signed. This followed @pshkv's detailed comparison of AgentID with SINT Protocol's Avatar Layer, emphasizing the overlap in Ed25519 identity and behavioral fingerprinting (CSML metric) and suggesting alignment of behavioral fingerprinting approaches.

### Capability Token Enforcement for Tool Calls
The "Plugin proposal: @sint/eliza-plugin — capability token enforcement for Eliza agent tool calls" issue saw further development [#6707](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6707). @pshkv provided a concrete implementation sketch for `@sint/eliza-plugin`, demonstrating how it would hook into Eliza's `IAgentRuntime` to enforce capability tokens before tool execution. The sketch included usage examples for `eliza.config.ts` and highlighted that the core policy engine and token verification are already built within the SINT Protocol monorepo. Questions were raised for maintainers regarding the preferred hook for integration, whether it should be a community plugin or standalone, and the next steps for implementation.
